questions.question: How does Vue implement scoped styles to prevent CSS from leaking to other components?
options: It wraps all component templates in a Shadow DOM root
It adds a unique `data-v-xxxxx` attribute to elements and appends it to each selector
It generates random class names and replaces all class references at build time
It uses CSS custom properties (variables) namespaced per component

questions.question: Why are CSS Modules a better choice than scoped styles for teleported content?
options: Scoped styles are not supported inside `<Teleport>` by Vue's compiler
CSS Modules hash class names globally so they work anywhere in the DOM, not just inside the component tree
Teleport requires class names to start with a specific prefix
CSS Modules automatically add `!important` to override teleport container styles
